name: matlab
tagline: Reference skill for writing MATLAB/Octave scripts: matrix ops, plotting, stats, ODEs, and signal processing, with links to deeper docs.
verdict: Clean, low-risk reference skill. No credential access, no network exfiltration, no obfuscation, no hidden or manipulative instructions. Only minor quality gaps: no negative-trigger guidance, no error-handling advice, and example code overwrites output files (results.csv, results.png) without checks. Nothing here endangers a user who installs it.

highlights.summary: No explicit negative-trigger guidance (when NOT to use, e.g. for non-numeric scripting tasks or other languages); a user has no signal on scope boundaries.
No explicit statement of what output the skill should produce (script vs. explanation vs. execution) beyond implied code generation; left to context.
No guidance on error handling / try-catch patterns, or what to do when Octave/MATLAB isn't installed beyond the install snippet (lines 24-33).
